name: PagerDuty MCP Server
what_it_does: Official PagerDuty MCP server enabling AI agents to manage incidents, on-call schedules, services, and escalation policies — turning agents into active participants in incident response.
use_cases: Creating and resolving incidents programmatically from agents
Querying on-call schedules to route issues to the right person
Listing and filtering active incidents by service or severity
Acknowledging and escalating incidents in automated workflows
Generating incident summaries and post-mortem data for agents
not_for: Real-time metric collection (use Prometheus/Datadog for that)
Non-incident use cases like project management or deployment
best_when: An AI agent needs to participate in incident response — triaging alerts, acknowledging pages, routing to the right person, or generating incident summaries.
avoid_when: You need metric collection, log aggregation, or deployment tracking — PagerDuty is incident management, not observability.

auth.notes: REST API key (user or account-level) for MCP server. OAuth 2.0 available for user-specific actions. API keys can be scoped to read-only for safe agent access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 14-day trial; free for small teams on Starter plan
pricing.notes: Requires active PagerDuty subscription. The MCP server itself is open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit is 960 req/min per account — shared across all API users, not per key
Incident IDs are alphanumeric strings (not integers) — easy to mix up in agent context
On-call schedule queries require knowing the schedule ID — discovery requires a separate call
Webhook payloads use a different schema than REST API responses — check type carefully
Resolving an incident requires it to be acknowledged first in some configurations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Strong security model. HTTPS enforced, read-only API keys available for safe monitoring agents. SOC 2 Type II certified.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Read-only API keys available. Strong access control for multi-team environments.
